truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 identified 6 vulnerabilities correctly but missed 14. Severity and CWE assignments show minor discrepancies for some findings.

Items.Notes: Identified format string vulnerability in log_debug_message, but severity was rated Medium instead of Critical.
Integer overflow in factorial computation (result *= i) was not mentioned in the previous answer.
Correctly identified use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush, but rated severity Medium instead of Critical.
Command injection via config validation (validator_cmd + config_file) was not explicitly found; previous answer focused on execute_admin_command.
Identified stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression, but rated severity Medium instead of High.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Correctly identified race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment with matching severity and CWE.
Logic bomb in authentication (login_attempts_ > 5 with EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE) was not mentioned.
Path traversal in configuration loading was not mentioned.
Identified heap overflow in expression parser, but rated severity High instead of Critical and CWE-120 instead of CWE-122.
Hardcoded administrator credentials (ADMIN_SECRET) were not mentioned.
Weak PRNG for session tokens was not mentioned.
Thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging was not mentioned.
Insecure temporary file handling was not mentioned.
Persistent authentication state was not mentioned.
Unhandled exception in variable parsing was not mentioned.
Integer underflow in memory-pool block split was not mentioned.
Unbounded recursion in parser was not mentioned.
Data race in result cache was not mentioned.
TOCTOU in config loading was not mentioned.
Integer division by zero in command routing was not mentioned.
